title: Named Journey Governance
description: Schema for a named journey's governance rules (e.g. Checkout Flow). A named journey owns a scope code and a set of citable rules; each rule may have per-step provisions, may inherit the generic journey rules via journey.extends, and may refine (supersede) a generic journey rule.

$schema.description: Path to this JSON Schema for IDE validation and autocomplete.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier for this journey (e.g. 'checkout-flow').

scope.description: Scope code that prefixes every rule id in this file (e.g. 'CHK').

extends.description: Always ['journey'] today — opts this journey into the generic journey-tier rules. The generator unions this journey's keywords onto those rules' triggers.

description.description: What this journey represents and why it needs journey-level governance.

steps.description: Ordered step definitions, referenced by rule provisions for human-readable labels.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier for this step (e.g. 'order-confirmation').

description.description: What happens in this step and what the user is trying to accomplish.

isFinalStep.description: True for the terminal step of the journey.

rules.description: The journey's governance rules — each a citable rule, validated holistically against the full composition of a step.

aiHints.description: Hints for AI agents consuming this journey.

keywords.description: Terms that trigger this journey's rules during intent matching. Become the rules' triggers in the generated index.

notes.description: Natural language guidance for AI agents on how to apply these rules holistically.

severity.description: How hard the rule pushes back. error = hard block; warning = flagged for review; info = advisory.

ruleId.description: Authored, stable rule citation (e.g. 'CHK-1'). Never reused once retired.

title.description: Short human sentence naming the rule.

refines.description: Id of a generic journey rule this rule supersedes (lex specialis). Where both apply, this one wins and the generic rule is suppressed.

statement.description: The binding text of the rule.

rationale.description: Why the rule exists — the prose justification.

appliesWhen.description: The trigger / jurisdiction. Common keys: journey, step, scope, always.

provisions.description: The same rule applied to specific steps (the decimal citation, e.g. CHK-1.3).

status.description: Mark a rule repealed: it stays in the authored YAML (its citation is retired, never reused) but is dropped from the generated governance index.

step.description: Id of the step this provision applies to.

labelHint.description: Suggested label text. Informational only — not enforced.

type.description: Whether the configuration must be present or must not appear.

variant.description: The specific variant this constraint targets. Omit to apply to all variants.

quantity.description: How many instances are permitted or required across the full step composition.
